headings: Section 12. Applications for Payment, Releases, Lien-Free Project
text: Section 27. Arbitration/Choice of Law. Any and all disputes, controversies, claims, or demands solely between Contractor and Subcontractor arising out of or relating in any manner to the Subcontract Work, the Subcontract, any provision of the Subcontract and/or a breach of the Subcontract or any provision of the Subcontract (a 'Dispute') shall  be  resolved  through  binding  arbitration  administered  by  the  American  Arbitration  Association  under  its Construction  Industry  Arbitration  Rules,  Regular  Track  (an  'Arbitration').  The  parties  hereby  agree  that:  (1)  a single  arbitrator  will  preside  over  the  Arbitration;  and  (2)  the  decision  of  that  single  arbitrator  will  be  final  and binding. The parties further agree that the Subcontract shall be governed in all respects by the laws of the State of Louisiana and, in reaching a decision, the arbitrator shall apply Louisiana law to all substantive issues. Moreover, the  arbitrator  shall  have  the  power  to  award  all  remedies  that  could  be  awarded  by  a  Louisiana court.  The Arbitration shall take place in the venue where the property is located and judgment on any award issued by the arbitrator may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. This arbitration provision (the 'Arbitration Provision')
